mission: Make Rydoo the operating system for business spend by helping companies capture employee expenses quickly, control travel and card spend before leakage happens, and close cleanly into finance systems.

businessModel: Subscription revenue from expense, travel, and finance automation workflows priced by active users and capability tier
Expansion revenue from higher-value control features such as smart audit, travel workflows, advanced analytics, and card-enabled spend controls
Partner-driven value through ERP, HRIS, travel, and card ecosystem integrations
Adjacent workflow expansion into AP and controller automation for finance teams standardizing spend operations
Retention economics driven by embedding Rydoo into finance close, policy, and employee mobile workflows
marketForces: Finance teams are expected to control spend tightly without adding back-office headcount
Employees expect receipt capture and approvals to feel mobile-first and nearly instant
Travel policy is increasingly judged on how much spend is steered before booking rather than only audited after the trip
Corporate cards are shifting the category toward real-time spend control and automated reconciliation
Controllers increasingly choose spend platforms based on ERP readiness, auditability, and multi-entity operability
keyPartners: Travel management companies and booking platforms
Card issuing, banking, and transaction feed partners
Tax, mileage, and regional policy data providers

designPrinciples: Keep employee capture, mobile UX, and manager approvals close enough that submission friction is measured and improved in one loop.
Keep policy, smart audit, and card reconciliation tightly linked because finance teams judge control quality on the combined outcome, not isolated modules.
Keep travel booking ingestion and trip policy connected because travel leakage is only controllable if booking data reaches the spend-control model early.
Preserve strong accounting, tax, and audit foundations because finance trust is the retention anchor in this category.
Use one data and integration layer across HR, ERP, travel, and cards so multi-entity customers do not create fragmented control models.

recommendedStructure.mission: Own receipt capture, mobile submission, report building, and manager approval experience.
Own policy automation, smart audit, budgets, and card reconciliation quality.
Own travel booking workflows, trip policy, duty of care, card program, and spend controls.
Own reimbursement, accounting sync, tax handling, analytics, and integration foundations.

operatingModelNotes: The category compounds when employee ease and finance control improve together; either side alone is not enough.
Travel and card workflows should not fragment from policy and accounting because customers buy a closed control loop.
Adjacent AP expansion should share finance and integration foundations rather than becoming a separate product stack.
